response.justification: The candidate is a strong senior individual contributor with genuine developer-tooling credentials — he authored test frameworks (Trial, Fluent-Asserts), built CI/dev-ops infrastructure, created testing courses, and currently works on AI-agent developer tooling — plus some mobile experience (iOS/Apple TV). However, this is a Level II people-management role requiring 2+ years of people management and 3+ years of formal technical leadership, and the resume shows no people-management experience: every role is an IC engineering position. Teaching workshops and founding a small open-source project do not substitute for managing engineers, setting performance expectations, or leading organizational change. He does not meet a core minimum requirement for the role.
strengths: Deep developer-tools background: test runners, assertion libraries, CI/CD infrastructure, and test automation courses
15+ years of broad software development experience across web, mobile, embedded, and high-throughput systems
Hands-on mobile-platform exposure (iOS and Apple TV apps shipped to top-10 US ranking)
concerns: No formal people-management experience — a hard minimum requirement (2+ years) for this manager role
No demonstrated experience leading organizational change or setting team-level roadmaps and performance expectations
Career trajectory is consistently IC-focused, suggesting a possible mismatch with a people-leadership position
